title: AWS Solutions Architect Associate
description: Question batch for the Design High-Performing Architectures domain.

questions.question: Which service improves application response time by caching data in memory?
questions.explanation: ElastiCache reduces latency for frequently requested data.

questions.question: Which AWS service reduces global latency for static content delivery?
questions.explanation: CloudFront caches content at edge locations closer to users.

questions.question: Which RDS feature is commonly used to scale read-heavy workloads?
questions.explanation: Read Replicas offload read traffic from the primary database.

questions.question: Which compute option is well suited for short event-driven workloads?
questions.explanation: AWS Lambda is optimized for event-driven, short-lived compute.

questions.question: Which service queries data in S3 using SQL without managing infrastructure?
questions.explanation: Amazon Athena provides serverless SQL queries for data in S3.

questions.question: Which service provides scalable shared file storage for multiple Linux EC2 instances?
questions.explanation: Amazon EFS provides shared, elastic file storage for Linux workloads.

questions.question: Which load balancing option is most appropriate for HTTP and HTTPS traffic?
questions.explanation: Application Load Balancers are optimized for Layer 7 web traffic.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of caching query results near users?
answers: Lower latency and reduced backend load
questions.explanation: Caching reduces repeated backend work and speeds up user responses.

questions.question: Which service is purpose-built for large-scale analytical warehousing?
questions.explanation: Amazon Redshift is AWS's managed data warehouse service.

questions.question: Which service helps choose better EC2 sizes based on workload patterns?
questions.explanation: Compute Optimizer recommends instance sizes based on observed usage.

questions.question: Which architecture choice best improves read performance for repeated product lookups?
questions.explanation: A cache layer reduces repeated reads against the primary data source.

questions.question: Which container service is best for Kubernetes workloads on AWS?
questions.explanation: Amazon EKS is the managed Kubernetes service on AWS.

questions.question: Which storage type is typically best for highly durable static web assets?
questions.explanation: Amazon S3 is durable and well suited for static asset storage.

questions.question: What is the main performance benefit of horizontal scaling?
answers: It increases capacity by adding more resources
It reduces the need for monitoring
It makes one server infinitely large
questions.explanation: Horizontal scaling distributes load across multiple resources.

questions.question: Which service improves resilience and performance by routing DNS queries globally?
questions.explanation: Route 53 provides scalable DNS with advanced routing capabilities.

questions.question: Which database service is best for single-digit millisecond NoSQL access at scale?
questions.explanation: DynamoDB is a managed NoSQL database designed for low-latency performance at scale.

questions.question: Which service helps stream large amounts of ordered data for near-real-time processing?
questions.explanation: Amazon Kinesis is used for ingesting and processing streaming data.

questions.question: Which architecture best supports high throughput for web traffic bursts?
answers: Auto Scaling behind a load balancer
questions.explanation: Elastic scaling with load balancing handles bursty traffic efficiently.

questions.question: Which design improves performance for globally distributed API consumers?
answers: Use CloudFront with edge caching where appropriate
Keep everything in one private subnet
questions.explanation: Edge caching can reduce latency for geographically dispersed users.

questions.question: Why are asynchronous patterns often good for high-performance systems?
answers: They reduce tight coupling and smooth workload spikes
questions.explanation: Asynchronous designs improve throughput and reduce blocking between services.

questions.question: Which design best improves database performance for read-heavy analytics without overloading OLTP traffic?
answers: Offload analytics to purpose-built services or replicas
Run analytics on the primary only
questions.explanation: Separating analytics and transactional workloads reduces contention.

questions.question: Which storage option is best for repeatedly accessed shared content across many instances?
answers: Amazon EFS or S3 depending access pattern
One local disk per server only
questions.explanation: Shared storage choices should align with access pattern and performance needs.

questions.question: Which practice most directly improves application performance troubleshooting?
answers: Use metrics, tracing, and logs together
questions.explanation: Observability data helps identify bottlenecks and regressions.

questions.question: Which approach best supports performance testing before production launch?
questions.explanation: Performance testing validates architecture behavior under expected load.

questions.question: Why are content delivery networks useful for performance?
answers: They reduce distance between users and cached content
They remove the need for storage
They force all traffic through one Region
questions.explanation: CDNs improve content delivery speed by caching close to users.

questions.question: Which design most directly helps prevent one slow downstream service from degrading the whole system?
questions.explanation: Decoupling with queues reduces the blast radius of slow components.

questions.question: Which compute model can be cost-effective and high-performing for unpredictable short-lived demand?
questions.explanation: Serverless compute can perform well for bursty short tasks without idle capacity cost.

questions.question: Which approach best supports low-latency repeat access to the same data objects?
questions.explanation: Caching hot data reduces the need to fetch from slower primary stores repeatedly.

questions.question: Which load balancer is most suitable for ultra-high-performance TCP traffic?
questions.explanation: Network Load Balancer is optimized for high-performance Layer 4 traffic.

questions.question: Which strategy best supports performance as user demand grows steadily?
answers: Use one monolith with fixed capacity
questions.explanation: Independent scaling lets each tier grow according to its specific demand.
